scenarios.name: Internet-facing remote access zero-day threatening EHR connectivity
scenarios.technical_signal: A critical remote code execution vulnerability in the VPN or remote access gateway used by clinical staff to access the EHR has been identified with active exploitation reported.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A remotely exploitable vulnerability on the clinical remote access gateway
recommended_actions: Patch or isolate the VPN gateway within 24 hours to protect EHR access paths.
Force credential reset for all clinical staff with active remote sessions.
Notify clinical leadership of potential impact to remote care delivery.

scenarios.name: Privileged identity compromise with lateral movement into clinical systems
scenarios.technical_signal: EDR telemetry shows suspicious PowerShell activity on a clinical administrator workstation followed by anomalous access to EHR databases and patient record exports.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A likely compromise of a privileged clinical identity with access to patient records
recommended_actions: Disable compromised accounts and revoke active EHR sessions immediately.
Audit patient record access logs for signs of unauthorised export.
Notify privacy officer and legal team for HIPAA breach assessment.

scenarios.name: Cloud misconfiguration exposing patient health information
scenarios.technical_signal: A cloud storage repository used for clinical research data exports has been found publicly accessible, exposing de-identified and potentially re-identifiable patient records.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A cloud misconfiguration exposing clinical research data to the public internet
recommended_actions: Remove public access to the research data repository immediately.
Assess whether the exposed data includes re-identifiable patient records.
Notify privacy officer and initiate HIPAA breach assessment within 24 hours.

scenarios.name: Ransomware outbreak threatening clinical operations and patient care continuity
scenarios.technical_signal: Ransomware has been detected encrypting clinical workstations and application servers, threatening continuity of EHR access and patient care operations.
scenarios.executive_trigger: Active ransomware encryption detected on clinical systems threatening patient care
recommended_actions: Activate downtime procedures and switch to paper-based workflows for patient care immediately.
Isolate affected clinical systems from the network to contain lateral spread.
Engage ransomware incident response retainer and notify hospital leadership.
Assess backup integrity and estimate EHR restoration timeline for executive briefing.

scenarios.name: Identity provider compromise enabling access to all clinical applications
scenarios.technical_signal: The hospital identity provider has been compromised, potentially enabling an attacker to forge authentication tokens and access the EHR, patient portal, and clinical imaging system without valid credentials.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A compromise of the centralised identity provider enabling unauthorised access to clinical applications
recommended_actions: Revoke all active SSO sessions across clinical applications immediately.
Rotate identity provider signing keys and audit recent authentication events.
Notify clinical application owners to begin compromise scope assessment.
Prepare HIPAA breach notification for potential patient data exposure.

scenarios.name: Email platform compromise exposing clinical communications
scenarios.technical_signal: The healthcare organisation's email platform has been compromised, exposing internal clinical communications, patient appointment data, and potentially PHI transmitted by email.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A compromise of the clinical email platform exposing protected health information
recommended_actions: Patch the email platform and reset credentials for all impacted accounts.
Audit email audit logs for signs of PHI exfiltration or forwarding rule tampering.
Notify privacy officer to assess HIPAA notification obligations.
Issue internal communication advisory on the risk of email-based impersonation.

scenarios.name: Patient portal API credential exposure enabling data access
scenarios.technical_signal: API keys or bearer tokens for the patient portal or clinical integration API are exposed, potentially granting access to patient appointment data, health records, or insurance eligibility responses.
scenarios.executive_trigger: Exposed API credentials enabling unauthorised access to patient-facing services
recommended_actions: Rotate all exposed API keys and bearer tokens immediately.
Audit API access logs for signs of unauthorised patient data queries.
Implement secrets scanning in integration pipelines.
Notify privacy officer of potential PHI exposure via API access.

scenarios.name: Unauthenticated clinical database exposure threatening patient data
scenarios.technical_signal: A clinical or research database instance is accessible without authentication, exposing patient health information, trial data, or operational records.
scenarios.executive_trigger: An unauthenticated clinical database exposed to the network
recommended_actions: Enforce authentication on the database and block unauthenticated access immediately.
Audit query logs for signs of data extraction.
Launch HIPAA breach assessment with privacy officer and legal.
Prepare patient notification plan if PHI exposure is confirmed.

scenarios.name: Medical device network attack threatening patient safety systems
scenarios.technical_signal: A cyber attack targeting the connected medical device network threatens the integrity of infusion pumps, patient monitoring systems, or other critical care devices.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A cyber attack on connected medical devices with patient safety implications
recommended_actions: Isolate affected medical device network segments and notify clinical engineering immediately.
Notify the CMO and patient safety officer of potential device integrity risk.
Engage biomedical engineering for device integrity assessment.
Notify device vendors and consider FDA reportable medical device cybersecurity event.

scenarios.name: Credential attack on clinical SaaS applications compromising patient access
scenarios.technical_signal: Credential stuffing or password spraying attacks are resulting in account takeover against the patient portal or clinical SaaS applications, potentially exposing patient health records.
scenarios.executive_trigger: Account takeover attacks on patient-facing and clinical SaaS applications
recommended_actions: Force password resets and enforce MFA on all affected patient and clinical accounts.
Block suspicious source IP ranges and implement adaptive authentication.
Audit recently accessed patient records for unauthorised access.
Notify privacy officer and assess HIPAA reportability of account takeover incidents.

scenarios.name: Database server vulnerability threatening clinical data integrity
scenarios.technical_signal: A critical vulnerability in the EHR database server allows remote code execution, threatening the integrity of patient health records and clinical workflow data.
scenarios.executive_trigger: A critical database vulnerability on the EHR backend
recommended_actions: Patch or isolate the affected database server on an emergency timeline.
Audit recent EHR data access for signs of unauthorised modification.
Notify CMO and privacy officer of potential patient data integrity risk.

scenarios.name: Outdated patient portal software expanding the attack surface
scenarios.technical_signal: The patient portal is running an outdated web server version with known vulnerabilities, increasing attack surface for patient-facing services.
scenarios.executive_trigger: An outdated internet-facing patient portal web server
recommended_actions: Upgrade the patient portal to a supported software version urgently.
Review WAF coverage for the patient-facing web tier.
Monitor for web exploitation attempts until patching is complete.
